findings.title: IIFE in JSX for dialog rendering is hard to follow
findings.why_it_matters: The `{conflictNoteId != null && (() => { ... })()}` pattern in JSX is non-idiomatic and visually noisy. Future maintainers may mistake it for a component or misread the null-safe logic. It's also fragile — the inner null check `conflictNote && conflictNote.conflictCopy != null` duplicates the outer `conflictNoteId != null` check.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract into a derived variable before the return: `const activeConflictNote = conflictNoteId != null ? notes.find(n => n.id === conflictNoteId) ?? null : null` and render `{activeConflictNote?.conflictCopy != null && <NoteConflictDialog note={activeConflictNote} ... />}`.
evidence: Notes.tsx lines 421-431: IIFE `{conflictNoteId != null && (() => { const conflictNote = notes.find(...); return conflictNote && conflictNote.conflictCopy != null ? ... : null })()`
The pattern is unique in the codebase — NoteCard.tsx uses a cleaner `{showConflictDialog && note.conflictCopy != null && <NoteConflictDialog .../>}` pattern for the same feature

findings.title: conflictCopy fieldMap entry is redundant with auto camelCase conversion
findings.why_it_matters: The explicit `conflictCopy: 'conflict_copy'` in fieldMap produces the same output as auto camelToSnake conversion. The comment says 'explicit entry ensures upload direction is correct' but fieldMapper.toSnakeCase already applies explicit entries first then falls back to camelToSnake — both produce 'conflict_copy'. The redundant entry adds cognitive overhead for future maintainers reviewing fieldMap intent.
findings.suggested_fix: Document the redundancy in the comment (it's harmless) or remove the explicit conflictCopy entry and keep only conflictSourceId (which correctly maps to conflict_source_id). The plan doc already flags this as known-redundant.
evidence: fieldMapper.ts line 86: `const mappedKey = entry.fieldMap[key] ?? camelToSnake(key)` — explicit wins, but camelToSnake('conflictCopy') = 'conflict_copy' which is identical
tableRegistry.ts line 130: `conflictCopy: 'conflict_copy'` — same as auto-conversion result
Plan doc docs/plans/2026-04-18-014-feat-e93-s03-note-conflict-preservation-plan.md explicitly notes: 'explicit fieldMap entry may be redundant — verify fieldMapper behavior'

residual_risks: The dual conflict badge implementations (NoteCard.tsx vs Notes.tsx) share no code and could diverge in behavior. If conflict resolution logic changes, both must be updated independently.
